disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began after a minor wrist injury 4 weeks ago, with abrupt severe deterioration over the past 12 hours; strictly unilateral, confined to one limb
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and was there any injury beforehand?
Has anything happened to that hand or wrist recently?
Tell me how this came on — was it sudden or gradual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started after I twisted my wrist about a month ago — only a small injury — but in the last 12 hours it's suddenly become horrendous. It's only the one hand.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Burning, throbbing pain with allodynia — even light brushing of the skin provokes severe pain
trigger_question_examples: What does the pain feel like — burning, aching, stabbing?
Does even a light touch or the bedsheet on your skin hurt?
Is the skin extremely sensitive to touch?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a burning, throbbing pain, and honestly even the lightest touch — a sheet, a breath of air — sends it through the roof.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Vasomotor instability — the hand fluctuates between dusky blue and red with a mottled (livedo) pattern and feels cold
trigger_question_examples: What does the hand look like — any colour or temperature change?
Is the colour of the skin changing at all?
Does the hand feel warm or cold compared with the other side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It keeps changing colour — goes a dusky blue then red and blotchy — and it feels freezing cold, much colder than my good hand.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors, or feeling systemically unwell — infective/systemic screen negative
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills or shivering?
Do you feel unwell in yourself, hot and cold, or sweaty with fevers?
Any temperature or feeling generally sick alongside the hand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no fevers or shivering. I checked my temperature and it was normal. I don't feel sick in myself, it's really just the hand.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really thought about it — I've just been focused on the pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The hand is not hot and not spreading-red — no advancing erythema, red streaking, or pus; skin is cool and dusky rather than inflamed-warm
trigger_question_examples: Is the hand hot to touch or is there redness spreading up the arm?
Any red streaks travelling up towards your elbow, or any pus?
Does the skin feel hot and inflamed, or cool?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's not hot — if anything it's cold. There's no redness creeping up my arm and no pus, it's just this blotchy blue-and-red colour.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure, I haven't looked that closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cold/ice application markedly worsens the pain
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it, and did it help or make it worse?
Did anything you tried, like heat or ice, change the pain?
What have you done so far to manage it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I put an ice pack on it thinking it would help, but it made the pain dramatically worse, so I took it straight off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe pain provoking nausea, presyncope-type dizziness, and a subjectively racing heart — a pain-driven autonomic response
trigger_question_examples: How is the pain affecting you — do you feel sick or faint with it?
Do you feel dizzy or like your heart is racing when the pain spikes?
Is the pain making you feel unwell in any other way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When the pain really spikes I feel sick and lightheaded, like I could faint, and my heart feels like it's racing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and cannot use the dominant hand — cannot open medication bottles, prepare food, or use the phone properly (functional and social-safety impact)
trigger_question_examples: Who is at home with you and are you managing okay day to day?
Are you able to look after yourself and take any medication safely?
Do you have anyone who can help you at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own, and I can't do anything with this hand — I can't even open the medication bottles or hold the phone properly.
